normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
inductive Tree : Type
| Nil : Tree
| Node : Tree → Int → Tree → Tree
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the same tree datatype as in the Rust implementation (Nil or Node(left, key, right)).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def bstInsert : Tree → Int → Tree
| Tree.Nil, key => Tree.Node Tree.Nil key Tree.Nil
| Tree.Node left k right, key =>
    if key < k then Tree.Node (bstInsert left key) k right
    else if k < key then Tree.Node left k (bstInsert right key)
    else Tree.Node left k right
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: State the Lean definition of bst insertion matching the Rust pattern matching and the three-way comparison (less, greater, equal).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem bstInsert_terminates (t : Tree) (key : Int) : True := by trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The recursive calls are on strict subtrees (left or right), so the function is well-founded/terminates. (In Lean, this is typically discharged by using a structurally recursive definition; here we record the obligation as a placeholder.)

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ key : Int,
  bstInsert Tree.Nil key = Tree.Node Tree.Nil key Tree.Nil
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: When inserting into an empty tree, the result is a single-node BST containing the key.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ left right : Tree, ∀ k key : Int,
  key < k →
  bstInsert (Tree.Node left k right) key = Tree.Node (bstInsert left key) k right
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the inserted key is less than the current node key, insertion recurses into the left subtree and leaves the right subtree unchanged.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ left right : Tree, ∀ k key : Int,
  k < key →
  bstInsert (Tree.Node left k right) key = Tree.Node left k (bstInsert right key)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the inserted key is greater than the current node key, insertion recurses into the right subtree and leaves the left subtree unchanged.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ left right : Tree, ∀ k key : Int,
  ¬(key < k) → ¬(k < key) →
  bstInsert (Tree.Node left k right) key = Tree.Node left k right
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the inserted key equals the current node key (i.e., neither less nor greater), insertion is a no-op: the tree is unchanged.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def BST : Tree → Prop
| Tree.Nil => True
| Tree.Node left k right =>
    BST left ∧ BST right ∧
    (∀ x, x ∈ keys left → x < k) ∧
    (∀ x, x ∈ keys right → k < x)

-- obligation (assuming a standard BST predicate over Int keys)
∀ t key, BST t → BST (bstInsert t key)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a binary search tree, then the output of bst insertion is also a BST.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def keys : Tree → Set Int
| Tree.Nil => ∅
| Tree.Node left k right => keys left ∪ {k} ∪ keys right

∀ t key,
  keys (bstInsert t key) =
    if key ∈ keys t then keys t else keys t ∪ {key}
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The set of keys in the tree is unchanged on duplicates; otherwise the inserted key is added.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ t key,
  (key ∈ keys t) → bstInsert t key = t
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the key already exists in the tree, insertion returns the original tree (immutable no-op on duplicates).
